LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO JULY-28-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GARY KAPLAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF KATHLEEN CRONINS RESPONSES TO DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. OFF CALENDAR. CCP 1005(B) STATES THAT "ALL MOVING AND SUPPORTING PAPERS SHALL BE SERVED AND FILED AT LEAST 16 COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING." DEFENDANT'S MOVING PAPERS WERE FILED ON JULY 15, 2025. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

DISCOVERY 301, DEFENDANT GARY KAPLAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF KATHLEEN CRONIN'S RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, IS GRANTED. THE COURT FURTHER AWARDS SANCTIONS. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA)

DISCOVERY 301, DEFENDANT GARY KAPLAN'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF KATHLEEN CRONIN'S DEPOSITION; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED. CRONIN SHALL APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION AT A MUTUALLY AGREED TIME WITHIN 20 DAYS OF THIS ORDER. CRONIN'S FAILURE TO APPEAR OR TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING PRIOR DEPOSITION NOTICES LACKS SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION AND CRONIN SHALL PAY $1091.50 TO DEFENDANT GARY KAPLAN WITHIN 30 DAYS. CRONIN IS CAUTIONED THAT FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH COURT ORDERS CONCERNING DISCOVERY MAY RESULT IN FURTHER MONETARY SANCTIONS OR EVIDENTIARY, ISSUE, OR TERMINATING SANCTIONS. PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA)

calendar.matters: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Kathleen Cronin?S Deposition; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Arpie A. Aslanian; Request For Sanctions Against Plaintiff; [Proposed] Order Of The Court
Off Calendar May-12-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GARY KAPLAN's Motion To Compel Plaintiff Kathleen Cronin's Deposition; Request For Sanctions Against Plaintiff.

The unopposed motion to compel the deposition of plaintiff Kathleen Cronin is granted. Cronin shall appear for deposition at a mutually agreed time within 20 days of this order. Cronin's failure to appear or to meet and confer regarding prior deposition notices lacks substantial justification and Cronin shall pay $1091.50 to defendant Gary Kaplan within 30 days. Cronin is cautioned that failure to comply with court orders concerning discovery may result in further monetary sanctions or evidentiary, issue, or terminating sanctions.

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-24-2026 Continued To Sep-14-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Kathleen Cronins Responses To Form Interrogatories
Off Calendar Aug-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GARY KAPLAN'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Kathleen Cronins Responses To Form Interrogatories

Defendant Gary Kaplan's motion to compel plaintiff Kathleen Cronin's responses to form interrogatories, set one, is granted. The court further awards sanctions.

Kaplan propounded this discovery to Cronin on April 16, 2025. (Aslanian Dec. para 3 & Exs. A & B.) Cronin has not responded. (Id. para. 8.)

By failing to timely respond, Cronin has waived her objections to the discovery requests. (CCP 2030.290(a).) Cronin is ordered to serve verified responses, without objections, within two weeks of notice of entry of this order.

Kaplan seeks sanctions. The court concludes that the failure to submit to an authorized method of discovery is a misuse of the discovery process and orders Cronin to pay $460 to Kaplan within 30 days of notice of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Kathleen Cronins Responses To Demand For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Aug-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - DEFENDANT GARY KAPLAN'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Kathleen Cronins Responses To Demand For Production Of Documents

Defendant Gary Kaplan's motion to compel plaintiff Kathleen Cronin's responses to requests for production of documents, set one, is granted. The court further awards sanctions.

Kaplan propounded this discovery to Cronin on April 16, 2025. (Aslanian Dec. para 3 & Exs. A & B.) Cronin has not responded. (Id. para. 8.)

By failing to timely respond, Cronin has waived her objections to the discovery requests. (CCP 2031.300(a).) Cronin is ordered to serve verified responses, without objections, accompanied by responsive documents, within two weeks of notice of entry of this order. If the responsive documents are voluminous Cronin may make a rolling production but shall complete production within 30 days.

Kaplan seeks sanctions. The court concludes that the failure to submit to an authorized method of discovery is a misuse of the discovery process and orders Cronin to pay $460 to Kaplan within 30 days of notice of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
